claim: Claude Code runs in the terminal, giving access to bash commands and file system that wouldn't be available in web or desktop interfaces
actionable: Use Claude Code CLI instead of IDE when you need agent access to system-level operations and terminal automation

claim: Claude Code is designed as a Unix utility that can be composed with other tools like tmux, not as a standalone IDE replacement
actionable: Compose Claude Code into existing Unix workflows (pipes, tmux, scripts) rather than replacing your entire development environment

claim: For knowledge base lookup, agentic search (letting Claude search the codebase iteratively) outperformed traditional RAG approaches across internal benchmarks
actionable: Use iterative agentic search instead of pre-indexed RAG for codebases to avoid sync and security issues

claim: Claude.md memory file is a simple markdown text file auto-loaded into context; you can place it in root, child directories, or home directory
actionable: Create a claude.md file in your project root to add persistent context Claude loads automatically

claim: Auto-compact feature automatically summarizes previous messages using Claude when context gets long, allowing effectively infinite context
actionable: Enable auto-compact to maintain long-running sessions without manually managing context window

claim: Approximately 80-90% of Claude Code's codebase is written by Claude itself, with significant human code review for critical paths
actionable: Use Claude to write most of your code, but reserve human review for intricate data modeling and code where you have strong opinions

claim: Costs are approximately $6/day per active user, which provides ROI when considering engineer salary and 50-70% productivity gains
actionable: Frame Claude Code costs as a productivity multiplier against engineer salary rather than a pure per-token cost

claim: Use /think command to explicitly ask Claude to plan and research before executing, or tell it 'make a plan, think, don't write code yet'
actionable: Before complex tasks, explicitly ask Claude to research and plan before implementation to avoid incorrect paths

claim: Custom /commands in Claude Code are just prompts saved locally; use them for simple workflows that don't need MCPs
actionable: Create local custom commands for frequently-used prompts instead of building full MCPs

claim: For non-interactive mode (-p flag), best practices are: start with read-only tasks, test on single item first, gradually scale up
actionable: When running Claude Code non-interactively, test on 1 item, then 10, then scale to thousands to catch failure patterns early

claim: The simple approach usually works well with good models; complex memory architectures and external solutions often add unnecessary overhead
actionable: Start with simple memory solutions (markdown files, hashtags for tagging) before investing in complex knowledge graphs or external stores

claim: Claude 3.7 Sonnet is more persistent in pursuing goals but sometimes takes literal interpretation; needs better common sense guidance
actionable: When using Claude 3.7, include explicit constraints in prompts to prevent over-literal interpretations (e.g., 'make test pass by fixing logic, not hardcoding')

claim: Permission system in Claude Code allows allowlisting/denylisting specific tools and actions (read/edit files, run tests, execute bash)
actionable: Configure permission allowlists for non-interactive mode using --allowed-tools flag with specific tools like 'bash' or 'get diff'

claim: Web fetch feature requires URLs to be in CLAUDE.md or directly provided by user; it won't follow URLs from arbitrary content to prevent injection attacks
actionable: Explicitly add trusted URLs to claude.md or pass them directly; don't rely on Claude fetching URLs found in fetched content

claim: Pre-commit hooks should complete in under 5 seconds; use fast checks (type, lint) in hooks and defer expensive operations to CI/GitHub Actions
actionable: Keep pre-commit hooks under 5 seconds by running only type-check and lint; move semantic linting to GitHub Actions

claim: People comfortable with prompt engineering get much more value from Claude Code than those less skilled at prompting
actionable: Invest in learning prompt engineering; the quality of your prompts directly determines the quality of Claude Code's work

claim: Use 'bun' for compilation and test running, not Node/npm, for faster build and test iteration with Claude Code
actionable: Replace npm with bun for test execution and compilation in projects using Claude Code

claim: You can run multiple Claude Code agents in parallel by asking Claude to 'research three separate ideas' to explore multiple solution paths
actionable: For complex decisions, ask Claude to explore 3-5 separate implementation approaches in parallel, then pick the best

claim: Resume across Claude Code sessions by having Claude write session state to a text doc, then read it back in a new session
actionable: For multi-session tasks, ask Claude to document the current state and goals in a text file; read it back when resuming
